title: crupina villosa
intro: Crupina villosa, also known as hoary crupina or crupina thistle, is a perennial thistle plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its spiny stems and yellow or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Crupina villosa include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues and respiratory problems. Crupina villosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
